Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mildly elevated creatinine and chloride, and HbA1c is slightly above normal, suggesting impaired glucose tolerance or early diabetes. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible renal impairment and/or poorly controlled diabetes. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further evaluation of renal function and glycemic control.
